page_content:
ENGL493 Writing Genres as Social Action (3 Credits)

A rhetorical genre studies approach to understanding the work that

texts do in the world. Examines issues of identity, power, and medium

as they relate to writing in various contexts. Students analyze the texts, context(s), and social significance of a public, professional, digital, and/or advanced academic genre and produce writing that meets, modifies, and subverts expectations.

Recommended: Satisfactory completion of the professional writing requirement (FSPW).

Restriction: Must have earned a minimum of 60 credits.

2025-2026 [updated 2025.04.17]
